subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Principle of Least Privilege
description: A 60-second lesson on The Principle of Least Privilege.

hook.text: A junior developer has admin access to the production database. What could go wrong? Everything.

buildup.text: Least privilege means giving users and services only the minimum permissions they need to do their job.

discovery.text: If an account is compromised, least privilege limits the blast radius. The attacker can only reach a small area.

twist.text: In practice, most teams give too many permissions because restricting access slows down development.

climax.text: The balance between security and productivity is the eternal struggle. Least privilege leans toward safety.

punchline.text: Give the minimum access needed. No more, no less.

quiz.question: Why is the principle of least privilege often violated in practice?
options: Restricting access slows development, so teams grant broad permissions
Cloud providers don't support fine-grained permission controls
Least privilege only applies to physical access, not digital systems
